title: Domain-specific language models pre-trained on construction management systems corpora
task: 文本分类/命名实体识别
contribution: 开发了首个CMS领域专用语料库，预训练BERT/RoBERTa模型，在文本分类和NER任务上F1分数分别提升5.9%和8.5%
pdfFile: Zhong and Goodfellow - 2024 - Domain-specific language models pre-trained on construction management systems corpora.pdf
markdownFile: Zhong and Goodfellow - 2024 - Domain-specific language models pre-trained on construction management systems corpora.md
abstract: This paper presents the first domain-specific corpus for Construction Management Systems (CMS), built from academic publications, and introduces an end-to-end pipeline for pre-training and fine-tuning domain-specific pre-trained language models. Four corpora with varying data cleaning strategies were constructed, and transfer learning was applied to pre-train BERT and RoBERTa models on these corpora, followed by fine-tuning on text classification and named entity recognition tasks.
methodology: The authors developed four CMS domain corpora from academic papers and pre-trained BERT and RoBERTa models using dynamic masked language modeling with transfer learning, then fine-tuned the best-performing models on downstream text classification and named entity recognition datasets.
results: Domain-specific pre-training improved F1 scores by 5.9% for text classification (best F1=0.754 with RoBERTa) and 8.5% for named entity recognition (best F1=0.956 with RoBERTa) compared to models pre-trained on general corpora. All transformer-based models significantly outperformed baseline models (Logistic Regression and BiGRU).
